content.full_text:
California
Fair Political
Practices Commission

September 11, 1989

Dana W. Reed
3151 Airway Avenue, Suite M-1
Costa Mesa, CA 92626

Re: Your Request for Informal
Assistance
Our File No. I-89-453

Dear M. Reed:

We have received your letter of July 26, 1989, seeking
written confirmation of the advice I provided to you in our
telephone conversation of July 19, 1989, regarding reporting
requirements for a non-profit corporation which sponsors a
political committee under the Political Reform Act of 1974.¹ This
is to confirm that your letter accurately reflects that advice.
However, I would like to clarify a point made in your letter.

In regards to reporting requirements of a sponsor, you stated
that the sponsor need not disclose contributions either to or from
a committee it sponsors, or expenditures made on the sponsored
committee's behalf, if all of three criteria were satisfied. In
addition to the three you listed, a responsible officer of the
sponsor, as well as the treasurer of the committee, must verify
the committee's campaign statements. (Regulation 18419(c)(4).)

A copy of your letter is enclosed for your convenience.

If you have any further questions, please contact me at (916)
322-5662.

Sincerely,

Kathryn E. Donovan
General Counsel

By: Kevin S. Braaten-Moen
Political Reform Consultant

Enclosure

¹ Government Code Sections 81000-91015. All statutory
references are to the Government Code unless otherwise indicated.
Commission regulations appear at 2 California Code of Regulations
Section 18000, et seq. All references to regulations are to Title
2, Division 6 of the California Code of Regulations.

428 J Street, Suite 800 • P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA 95804-0807 • (916)322-5660

89-455

REED & DAVIDSON
ATTORNEYS AT LAW

DANA W. REED
CARY DAVIDSON

DARRYL R. WOLD
OF COUNSEL

3151 AIRWAY AVENUE, SUITE M-1
COSTA MESA, CALIFORNIA 92626
TELEPHONE (714) 641-668
FACSIMILE (714) 546-1003

FPPC

JUL 31 1989

888 SOUTH FIGUEROA, SUITE 2020
LOS ANGELES, CALIFORNIA 90017
TELEPHONE (213) 362-9236

July 26, 1989


Ms. Jeanne Pritchard
Division Chief, Technical Assistance and Analysis Division
Fair Political Practices Commission
428 J Street, Suite 800
Sacramento, California 95814

Dear Ms. Pritchard:

This letter requests informal assistance under Government Code
Section 83114(b). We request written confirmation of advice this
firm received from Kevin Bratten-Moen on July 19, 1989 concerning
reporting requirements for a non-profit corporation which
sponsors a political committee. I am requesting this advice as
Treasurer of the committee and it relates to a possible amendment
of previously filed reports. (2 California Code of Regulations,
Section 18329(c)(4)(A)).

Specifically, we were advised as follows: a committee sponsor
need not report contributions, including non-monetary
contributions, and expenditures on behalf of the committee it
sponsors if the sponsored committee reports such receipts from
the sponsor; and that a sponsor need not report the transfer of
funds to the sponsor from the sponsored committee if the
sponsored committee reports such a transfer to the sponsor.

The advice received was based on these facts. The sponsor is a
non-profit corporation, qualified under section 501(c)(4) of the
Internal Revenue Code. It does not engage in any campaign
activity itself, other than through one or more sponsored
committees. The sponsor does not make or receive contributions
or independent expenditures sufficient to qualify as a committee
under Government Code, Section 82013. Following the election for
which the sponsored committee was organized, the sponsor received
the surplus funds in a series of payments from the sponsored
committee. The funds paid to the sponsor by the sponsored
committee were in excess of $10,000.

Ms. Jeanne Pritchard
Page 2


A sponsor need not disclose contributions either to or from a
committee it sponsors, or expenditures made on the sponsored
committee's behalf, if all of the following criteria are
satisfied: the sponsor does not make or receive sufficient
contributions, including non-monetary contributions, or
expenditures to otherwise qualify as a committee, other than
those in support of its sponsored committee; the sponsored
committee reports all contributions or expenditures in support of
the committee by the sponsor; and the sponsored committee reports
contributions received from the sponsor as from an intermediary,
if required. (2 California Code of Regulations, Section 18419
(c)).

If you have any questions, please feel free to call.

                                        Sincerely,

                                        [illegible]
                                        Dana W. Reed

DWR:1b

sections.question: A committee sponsor need not report contributions, including non-monetary contributions, and expenditures on behalf of the committee it sponsors if the sponsored committee reports such receipts from the sponsor; and that a sponsor need not report the transfer of funds to the sponsor from the sponsored committee if the sponsored committee reports such a transfer to the sponsor. Specifically, whether a sponsor need not disclose contributions either to or from a committee it sponsors, or expenditures made on the sponsored committee's behalf, if all specified criteria are satisfied.
sections.conclusion: A sponsor need not disclose contributions either to or from a committee it sponsors, or expenditures made on the sponsored committee's behalf, if all of the following criteria are satisfied: the sponsor does not make or receive sufficient contributions, including non-monetary contributions, or expenditures to otherwise qualify as a committee, other than those in support of its sponsored committee; the sponsored committee reports all contributions or expenditures in support of the committee by the sponsor; and the sponsored committee reports contributions received from the sponsor as from an intermediary, if required. Additionally, a responsible officer of the sponsor, as well as the treasurer of the committee, must verify the committee's campaign statements.
sections.question_synthetic: Under the Political Reform Act of 1974, when must a non-profit corporation that sponsors a political committee report contributions, expenditures, and fund transfers between itself and the sponsored committee?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: A non-profit sponsor need not report contributions, expenditures, or fund transfers with its sponsored committee if three criteria are met: the sponsor qualifies as a committee only through its sponsored committees, the sponsored committee reports all such transactions, and the sponsored committee properly identifies the sponsor as an intermediary. A fourth requirement was clarified: both the sponsor's responsible officer and the committee's treasurer must verify the committee's campaign statements.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document consists of both incoming request (July 26, 1989 letter) and FPPC response (September 11, 1989 letter). Extracted information from the FPPC response as is_fppc_response=true. The response confirms previous telephone advice and adds clarification regarding verification requirements. File number 1-89-453 referenced.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: A committee sponsor need not report contributions, including non-monetary contributions, and expenditures on behalf of the committee it sponsors if the sponsored committee reports such receipts from the sponsor; and that a sponsor need not report the transfer of funds to the sponsor from the sponsored committee if the sponsored committee reports such a transfer to the sponsor. Specifically, whether a sponsor need not disclose contributions either to or from a committee it sponsors, or expenditures made on the sponsored committee's behalf, if all specified criteria are satisfied.

CONCLUSION: A sponsor need not disclose contributions either to or from a committee it sponsors, or expenditures made on the sponsored committee's behalf, if all of the following criteria are satisfied: the sponsor does not make or receive sufficient contributions, including non-monetary contributions, or expenditures to otherwise qualify as a committee, other than those in support of its sponsored committee; the sponsored committee reports all contributions or expenditures in support of the committee by the sponsor; and the sponsored committee reports contributions received from the sponsor as from an intermediary, if required. Additionally, a responsible officer of the sponsor, as well as the treasurer of the committee, must verify the committee's campaign statements.

embedding.summary: FPPC confirmed advice regarding reporting requirements for a non-profit corporation sponsoring a political committee under the Political Reform Act, clarifying that the sponsor need not report certain contributions and expenditures if specified criteria are satisfied, including verification by both the sponsor's officer and the committee's treasurer.
